title: [WIP] pkg read containers.conf

comments.body:
@TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

@QiWang19 Please implement one feature perhaps SELinux in buildah to read the config and use it.
containers.conf lives in common-files right?  Should the man page live there too?
A number of nitty doc comments.  I think this should live in c/common-files rather then here in Buildah.  Granted we'd need to do more work there for packaging, test setup, etc.....

Let's continue to work with this hear, and maybe we can move the code and man pages over to container-common.  We currently don't have code in  https://github.com/containers/common-files, but maybe it makes a good idea to start putting code there?  Maybe we could change the name of the repo to https://github.com/containers/common and then allow people to vendor from there, mainly config files.

I'll let others weigh in, but my vote would be to not include this in Buildah.  I'd just fear the inertia of having it there and the cost of moving it out eventually.  i.e. it won't move.  I really think we should take the hit now and get this in containers/common-files or a new containers/common repo as you suggest.  My $.02

I've used the package to read some containers conf in run_linux.go AdditionalDevices, Sysctls... 
Should containers.conf overrides the options from CLI?
Need to pass a path of containers.conf to add tests. `buildah --configpath`?

> I've used the package to read some containers conf in run_linux.go AdditionalDevices, Sysctls...
> Should containers.conf overrides the options from CLI?
> Need to pass a path of containers.conf to add tests. `buildah --configpath`?

@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
CLI should override containers.conf, but if we are displaying a default in the --help, then the default should come from containers.conf or hard coded defaults in the code.
Once DefaultConfig("") is created, we need to have Unit tests using different config files and make sure that the code works correctly.

> CLI should override containers.conf, but if we are displaying a default in the --help, then the default should come from containers.conf or hard coded defaults in the code.

current value of container.conf is passed to builder in func (b *Builder) Run run_linux.go, the b is already had values from CLI default or user specified. When do we need to update the b with containers.conf? 
for slice CLI options, append the containers.conf value to it.
for each string or boolean value, we need to check b.option != default value and then update it with containers.conf?

is this func (b *Builder) Run run_linux.go the right place to pass the value from containers.conf?

add few test tests/containers_conf.bats https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1858/files#diff-7740e6e05051df18cbea902bf748406e.. am I on the right path to continue? @rhatdan
@QiWang19  I will look at this later today and will be back to work tomorrow.  Please rebase.
